content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 30, 1976

A-76-456

Mr. Christopher M. Ames
Deputy Attorney General
Attorney General's Office
350 McAllister Street, Room 6000
San Francisco, CA 94102

Dear Chris:

     This letter will confirm our telephone conversation
of Tuesday, November 30, 1976, concerning the appropriate filing procedures for members of the California Coastal Zone Conservation Commission and the regional coastal zone conservation commissions whose terms will expire on December 31, 1976. In particular, we discussed the appropriate procedures to be employed by those members who will be reappointed to the California Coastal Commission and the various regional commissions.

     I indicated to you that every member of a commission
should file a leaving office Statement of Economic Interests
(Form 721) within 30 days after leaving office or, in other
words, by January 30, 1977. The only exception to this procedure will be those members who are reappointed and who wish
to assume office prior to January 30, 1977. Those individuals
should file their leaving office statements prior to assuming
office.

     If an individual follows the foregoing procedure, it
will not be necessary to file an assuming office Statement of
Economic Interests (Form 720) when he or she is reappointed to
the California Coastal Commission or one of the regional commissions. The leaving office statement will suffice as an
assuming office statement.

     Accordingly, to recapitulate, individuals who are
reappointed after January 30, 1977, need only file one Statement
of Economic Interests (Form 721) no later than January 30, 1977,
and that statement will suffice as an assuming office statement.
Individuals who are reappointed prior to January 30, 1977, also
need only file one Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721),
but must do so prior to assuming office and, in any event, no
later than January 30, 1977.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

This image appears to be a mostly blank page with minimal visible text content. I can see two curved marks or symbols near the top of the page that resemble parentheses or crescent shapes, but the rest of the page is essentially empty or too faint to read clearly.

Mr. Christopher W. Ames
Page Two
November 30, 1976


     Finally, we discussed the fact that for the anniversary
statements to be filed in 1978 by reappointed members, the period
covered shall commence with January 1, 1977, the day following
the closing date for the statements filed pursuant to the proce-
dure outlined above.

     If you have any further questions about this matter,
please do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth Finney
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

KF:jo

cc: Floyd Shimomura


Since there is no actual text content to transcribe in this image, I cannot provide a text transcription following your rules.
